title: Faceless YouTube
description: Create YouTube videos without showing your face.

meta.outcome: A fully finished, voice-guided YouTube video ready for upload.

bestFor.reason: Create high-quality content without being on camera.
Scale video production across multiple channels quickly.

steps.desc: Use ChatGPT to write high-retention video scripts and outlines.
steps.goal: Write a clear, structured script with a strong opening hook.
steps.whyNow: A good voiceover needs a good script. Always start with the narrative.
steps.expectedOutput: A formatted script with dialogue and visual cues.
steps.expertTip: Ask ChatGPT to separate your script into 'Hook', 'Intro', 'Body', and 'Call to Action'.
steps.commonMistake: Publishing the first draft. Always rewrite the intro in your own voice so it doesn't sound robotic.

steps.desc: Generate realistic human voiceovers using ElevenLabs.
steps.goal: Turn your written script into natural-sounding audio.
steps.whyNow: Audio sets the pacing for your video edit. You need the voiceover before you start adding B-roll.
steps.expectedOutput: An MP3 or WAV file of the spoken script.
steps.expertTip: Use the 'Stability' slider to dial in the emotion (lower stability means a more expressive voice).
steps.commonMistake: Ignoring mispronunciations. Use phonetic spelling if the AI struggles with specific words.

steps.desc: Combine your voice clips, stock footage, and subtitles in CapCut.
steps.goal: Assemble the final video so it flows well and keeps attention.
steps.whyNow: Now that you have the story and pacing, you can bring it to life visually.
steps.expectedOutput: A final rendered MP4 file.
steps.expertTip: Use CapCut's 'Auto Captions' feature and color-code keywords to keep viewers engaged.
steps.commonMistake: Adding too many effects. Keep it clean so the visuals don't distract from what you're actually saying.

steps.desc: Find the right keywords, titles, and tags with TubeBuddy.
steps.goal: Make sure your video actually shows up in YouTube search.
steps.whyNow: The video is done. Now you need the metadata to get it in front of people.
steps.expectedOutput: An optimized title, description, and keyword list.
steps.expertTip: Look for keywords with 'Fair' or 'Good' overall scores—high search volume but manageable competition.
steps.commonMistake: Publishing without checking if people are actually searching for your topic.

whyThisOrder.explanation: This follows the classic video production timeline: Scripting comes first, followed by audio, then video editing, and finally distribution.
whyThisOrder.impact: If you try to edit video without a locked-in script or voiceover, you'll end up re-editing the same footage over and over.

budgetMode.description: A completely free pipeline for beginners just starting their channel.

premiumMode.description: The professional stack for maximum retention and channel growth.

faqs.question: Do I need a paid ElevenLabs account?
faqs.answer: For commercial use (monetizing on YouTube), yes. However, you can test the workflow on the free tier.

faqs.question: Can I use AI generated images for B-roll?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Tools like Midjourney or Leonardo AI can generate custom visuals to use in CapCut.

title: AI Influencer
description: Create realistic AI influencers for social media.

meta.outcome: High-quality, consistent images and videos of a custom digital persona.

bestFor.reason: Create brand ambassadors without hiring talent.
Maintain high posting volume with a consistent face.

steps.desc: Generate consistent, high-fidelity model images in Midjourney using seed values.
steps.goal: Create a consistent and recognizable character base.
steps.whyNow: Visual identity must be established before you can create content or write a persona.
steps.expectedOutput: A master sheet of images with identical facial features.
steps.expertTip: Use the '--cref' and '--cw' parameters in Midjourney v6 to maintain character consistency.
steps.commonMistake: Changing the prompt too much between shots, resulting in a character that looks like different people.

steps.desc: Establish the influencer's backstory and write social posts with ChatGPT.
steps.goal: Give the character a compelling, relatable personality.
steps.whyNow: Once you have the look, you need the voice to match before creating your final posts.
steps.expectedOutput: A brief brand guideline and 10 ready-to-post captions.
steps.expertTip: Set up a custom GPT with your influencer's backstory to keep the tone consistent across all posts.
steps.commonMistake: Making the personality too perfect. Real people have flaws—add some to make them relatable.

steps.desc: Animate static character images into short video clips with Runway Gen-2.
steps.goal: Bring the static character to life for short-form video.
steps.whyNow: Video drives higher engagement than photos, but it needs to be built off your static visual foundation.
steps.expectedOutput: 3-5 second realistic looping videos.
steps.expertTip: Use the 'Motion Brush' tool to isolate movement—like making just the hair or clothing sway.
steps.commonMistake: Pushing the motion slider too high, causing the face to distort.

steps.desc: Format your images and stories for social platforms using Canva.
steps.goal: Package everything into polished, native-looking social posts.
steps.whyNow: The raw images and video clips need typography and branding before they go live.
steps.expectedOutput: A week's worth of scheduled visual content.
steps.expertTip: Use Canva's Bulk Create feature with a CSV of your captions to churn out 30 posts in minutes.
steps.commonMistake: Using mismatched fonts and colors across different posts.

whyThisOrder.explanation: Nailing the visual consistency (Midjourney) is the hardest part. Once you have that, the persona (ChatGPT), movement (Runway), and formatting (Canva) fall into place naturally.
whyThisOrder.impact: If you try writing captions before you know what the character looks like, the tone usually feels disconnected from the aesthetic.

budgetMode.description: A zero-cost setup utilizing daily free credits to build the persona slowly.

premiumMode.description: Uncapped generation capabilities for running multiple influencer accounts.

faqs.question: Do I have to disclose it's an AI influencer?
faqs.answer: Yes, many platforms (like TikTok and Meta) now require AI-generated content labels to prevent user deception.

faqs.question: How hard is it to keep the face consistent?
faqs.answer: Much easier now than a year ago! Midjourney's Character Reference (--cref) parameter makes it extremely reliable.

title: Vibe Coding
description: Build apps faster using AI coding assistants.

meta.outcome: A fully functional web application deployed live on a public URL.

bestFor.reason: Build and launch MVPs in days instead of months.
Prototype functional features without waiting on engineering.

steps.desc: Map out your app's architecture and data models with Claude 3.
steps.goal: Get a clear technical plan on paper before writing any code.
steps.whyNow: Jumping into code without a plan usually leads to messy, hard-to-maintain code.
steps.expectedOutput: A markdown file outlining your tech stack, schema, and basic API routes.
steps.expertTip: Paste your existing package.json or database schema into Claude and ask it to write the spec around your current constraints.
steps.commonMistake: Skipping the planning phase and asking the AI to 'build the whole app' in one prompt.

steps.desc: Generate your React components quickly with v0 by Vercel.
steps.goal: Spin up a clean, responsive frontend layout.
steps.whyNow: Once you have the visual structure, wiring up the backend logic is much easier.
steps.expectedOutput: Copy-pasteable React and Tailwind code.
steps.expertTip: Upload a screenshot of a UI you like—v0 is surprisingly good at recreating it.
steps.commonMistake: Trying to force v0 to handle complex business logic. Stick to using it for UI.

steps.desc: Write logic and debug in Cursor.
steps.goal: Connect your frontend to your backend and fix any bugs.
steps.whyNow: Now that you have a plan and the UI, Cursor is the best place to do the actual heavy lifting.
steps.expectedOutput: A working, bug-free codebase on your local machine.
steps.expertTip: Use Cursor's '@Codebase' feature to give the AI context about your entire project directory, not just the file you're in.
steps.commonMistake: Forgetting to commit to Git frequently. AI can make sweeping changes that you might need to roll back.

steps.desc: Use GitHub Copilot for fast inline suggestions.
steps.goal: Speed up the manual typing and boilerplate generation.
steps.whyNow: This runs constantly in the background while you work in Cursor.
steps.expectedOutput: Faster typing and fewer syntax errors.
steps.expertTip: Write a descriptive comment like '// function to parse JWT token' and wait a second—Copilot will usually write it for you.
steps.commonMistake: Blindly hitting 'Tab' without reading what Copilot actually suggested.

whyThisOrder.explanation: Planning (Claude) → UI Scaffolding (v0) → Core Execution (Cursor) → Micro-refinements (Copilot).
whyThisOrder.impact: If you execute without a solid plan, you'll burn through your AI token limits quickly and end up with code hallucinations.

budgetMode.description: A highly capable setup that leverages the free tiers of the best tools.

premiumMode.description: The ultimate 10x developer stack with maximum context windows and speed.

faqs.question: Do I need to know how to code?
faqs.answer: You need a basic understanding of logic, terminal commands, and how web apps work, but you don't need to memorize syntax anymore.

faqs.question: Why use Cursor instead of VS Code?
faqs.answer: Cursor is a fork of VS Code built specifically for AI, offering features like multi-file edits and codebase-wide context that standard extensions can't match.

title: Content Creator
description: Plan, design and publish content faster.

meta.outcome: A week's worth of optimized social media posts and short-form video clips scheduled.

bestFor.reason: Repurpose long-form videos into dozens of Shorts/Reels.
Maintain an active social presence with minimal weekly effort.

steps.desc: Brainstorm copy, hooks, and captions for multiple platforms with ChatGPT.
steps.goal: Write a week's worth of engaging text content.
steps.whyNow: Content needs a message. Writing the copy first ensures your visuals align with the core idea.
steps.expectedOutput: A document with 7 distinct post ideas and captions.
steps.expertTip: Feed ChatGPT your top performing past posts and ask it to extract the framework to write new ones.
steps.commonMistake: Using overly formal, AI-sounding language. Tell it to write in a 'conversational, punchy' tone.

steps.desc: Design posts, carousels, and thumbnails in Canva.
steps.goal: Create eye-catching visuals that stop the scroll.
steps.whyNow: You need visuals to pair with the copy you just wrote.
steps.expectedOutput: Exported PNG/JPG graphics sized for specific platforms.
steps.expertTip: Use the 'Magic Switch' tool to instantly resize an Instagram square post into a Pinterest Pin or Story.
steps.commonMistake: Cluttering the design. Try to keep text on images under 20% of the total area.

steps.desc: Turn long-form videos into viral clips using Opus Clip.
steps.goal: Get the most out of your existing video content.
steps.whyNow: Short-form video is the best way to grow right now, and this takes minutes instead of hours.
steps.expectedOutput: Vertical videos with burned-in captions.
steps.expertTip: Review the clips before exporting—you can manually adjust the start/stop times if the AI missed the punchline.
steps.commonMistake: Publishing clips without double-checking the auto-captions for spelling errors.

steps.desc: Queue up your posts using Buffer.
steps.goal: Post consistently without needing to log in every day.
steps.whyNow: The final step: getting the content you just made in front of an audience.
steps.expectedOutput: A fully scheduled content calendar.
steps.expertTip: Look at your analytics and schedule posts for when your specific audience is online, not just the general 'best times'.
steps.commonMistake: Forgetting to tag relevant accounts or update the link in your bio.

whyThisOrder.explanation: Text/Strategy (ChatGPT) → Static Visuals (Canva) → Dynamic Assets (Opus Clip) → Distribution (Buffer).
whyThisOrder.impact: If you try to design graphics before writing the copy, you'll spend hours resizing text boxes because the words never fit the layout.

budgetMode.description: Perfect for new creators managing 3 or fewer social channels.

premiumMode.description: Unlocks brand kits, premium templates, and advanced analytics.

faqs.question: Can I use this for a Faceless channel?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can swap Opus Clip for a text-to-video generator if you don't have existing long-form content.

faqs.question: Does scheduling hurt reach?
faqs.answer: No, platforms officially support scheduling via API. Content quality matters far more than how it was published.

title: Agency
description: Run your client business with AI.

meta.outcome: Automated onboarding, tracking pipelines, and highly organized client project spaces.

bestFor.reason: Streamline operations to focus more on billable creative work.
Provide a highly professional, white-glove client experience automatically.

steps.desc: Set up your CRM, SOPs, and client portals in Notion AI.
steps.goal: Build a single, organized place for all your business operations.
steps.whyNow: You need a solid database before you can automate anything.
steps.expectedOutput: A workspace with linked databases for clients, projects, and tasks.
steps.expertTip: Use Notion AI to automatically summarize your meeting notes and pull action items directly into your CRM.
steps.commonMistake: Overcomplicating the databases early on. Start simple and add new properties only when you actually need them.

steps.desc: Draft proposals and handle client emails using ChatGPT.
steps.goal: Cut down on the hours spent writing repetitive client emails.
steps.whyNow: With your CRM in place, you now need to efficiently communicate that information.
steps.expectedOutput: Email templates and drafted proposals ready for review.
steps.expertTip: Set up a 'Custom Instruction' that explains your agency's tone, services, and pricing so ChatGPT doesn't guess.
steps.commonMistake: Sending AI emails without reading them first. Clients can easily spot a generic, unedited response.

steps.desc: Connect your apps and trigger actions with Zapier Central.
steps.goal: Stop copying and pasting data between different platforms.
steps.whyNow: Automation is the glue that connects your CRM, emails, and task manager.
steps.expectedOutput: Active Zaps (e.g., 'When a Typeform is submitted, create a Notion page and ping Slack').
steps.expertTip: Use Zapier's built-in AI formatter to clean up messy data before pushing it into your CRM.
steps.commonMistake: Creating infinite loops by accidentally having two Zaps trigger each other.

steps.desc: Track client progress and manage daily tasks in ClickUp.
steps.goal: Keep your team on track and actually execute the work.
steps.whyNow: This is where the actual work gets done, fed automatically by the tools you just set up.
steps.expectedOutput: A project dashboard with automated status updates.
steps.expertTip: ClickUp Brain can instantly answer questions about project statuses, saving you from interrupting your team.
steps.commonMistake: Creating too many sub-tasks. If the system is too complicated, your team simply won't use it.

whyThisOrder.impact: Building automations before you have a clear CRM structure will just result in broken Zapier workflows.

budgetMode.description: Robust enough to handle your first 5-10 clients at near-zero cost.

premiumMode.description: Enterprise-grade automation with unlimited logic paths and custom branding.

faqs.question: Should I use Make instead of Zapier?
faqs.answer: Make is significantly cheaper and more visual, but Zapier integrates with more obscure tools out-of-the-box.

faqs.question: Is client data secure in these AI tools?
faqs.answer: Always review Enterprise agreements. For high-security clients, ensure you opt-out of data training in ChatGPT and Notion.

title: Solopreneur
description: Launch and grow an online business with AI.

meta.outcome: A professional landing page with live payment integration, ready to accept customers.

bestFor.reason: Quickly spin up sales pages for new info-products.
Validate an idea and collect pre-orders before building the product.

steps.desc: Write your landing page copy and value props using Claude 3.
steps.goal: Draft a clear, convincing argument for why someone should buy your product.
steps.whyNow: Design needs to serve the copy. Writing the text first ensures your website actually makes sense.
steps.expectedOutput: A wireframe document with headlines, sub-headlines, and button text.
steps.expertTip: Claude 3 is great at nailing tone. Ask it to write using the 'Problem-Agitation-Solution' (PAS) framework.
steps.commonMistake: Focusing too much on technical features instead of the actual benefits to the customer.

steps.desc: Create logos and marketing graphics in Canva.
steps.goal: Give your new business a clean, trustworthy look.
steps.whyNow: You need visual assets ready to plug into your website builder.
steps.expectedOutput: A logo, color palette, and a few supporting images.
steps.expertTip: Set up Canva's Brand Hub with your colors and fonts so you can instantly apply them to any template.
steps.commonMistake: Spending three hours perfecting a logo for an idea you haven't even validated yet.

steps.desc: Build a fast, responsive landing page in Framer.
steps.goal: Get your site live on the internet without writing code.
steps.whyNow: This brings your copy and your visuals together into the final product.
steps.expectedOutput: A live website on a custom domain.
steps.expertTip: Framer has an AI generator—type in a prompt and it will give you a baseline layout that you can tweak.
steps.commonMistake: Forgetting to check the mobile view. Most of your traffic will probably come from phones.

steps.desc: Set up payment processing with Stripe.
steps.goal: Actually make money from your traffic.
steps.whyNow: The very last step to turn your project into a real business.
steps.expectedOutput: A live checkout link connected to your website's buy buttons.
steps.expertTip: Don't overcomplicate it. Just use Stripe Payment Links and attach the URL directly to your Framer buttons.
steps.commonMistake: Forgetting to configure tax settings before you launch.

whyThisOrder.impact: If you design a site before writing the copy, you'll end up with awkward text that doesn't fit the layout.

budgetMode.description: Launch an MVP for absolutely $0 upfront cost.

premiumMode.description: The professional standard to build trust and scale effectively.

faqs.question: Do I need an LLC to use Stripe?
faqs.answer: No, you can start as a Sole Proprietor in most countries, though forming an LLC is recommended later for liability protection.

faqs.question: Is Framer better than Webflow?
faqs.answer: Framer is generally faster to learn for designers (similar to Figma) and excellent for landing pages, while Webflow is better for complex CMS sites.
